project_name: English-First Cờ tướng (Vietnamese Chess) Web App (iPhone-First)

tech_stack.styling: Tailwind CSS (using 100dvh for mobile Safari UI)
tech_stack.state_management: Zustand (using FEN-like strings for lightweight state snapshots)
tech_stack.board_rendering: Responsive SVG board + GPU-accelerated CSS transforms (translate) for pieces
tech_stack.deployment: Vercel or GitHub Pages (Early deployment in Sprint 1 for physical device testing)

goals: Initialize Vite project with Tailwind CSS using h-[100dvh] for the main layout
Configure meta viewport tag with touch-action: manipulation (preserves pinch-to-zoom accessibility while preventing double-tap zoom)
Implement an auto-scaling SVG Cờ tướng board with proper ARIA labels for accessibility
Define internal coordinate system mapped to screen positions using CSS transform: translate(x, y)
Create minimum 44x44px invisible tap targets for each intersection to support finger input
Deploy MVP to Vercel/GitHub Pages for immediate physical iPhone testing of the bottom sheet layout
sprint_plan.deliverable: A static, accessible, auto-scaling board deployed live to verify touch targets and 100dvh layout.
sprint_plan.changes_from_original: Replaced user-scalable=0 with touch-action: manipulation for better accessibility.

goals: Implement tap-to-select and tap-to-move interaction (primary input method)
Stub pointerdown/pointermove handlers on pieces for future drag-and-drop (disabled by default, wired to CSS translate)
Add optional 'Confirm Move' toggle: select -> target -> confirm
Implement pseudo-legal move generation for Chariot, General, Advisor, Soldier
Add touch-optimized legal-move highlights and an ARIA-announced 'selected piece' callout
Add English illegal-move feedback via bottom-sheet toasts (e.g., 'Blocked path')
Add first Playwright mobile smoke test: board renders, tap selects piece, legal moves highlight
sprint_plan.deliverable: A touch-first board where basic pieces move smoothly using GPU acceleration, with Playwright test baseline established.
sprint_plan.changes_from_original: Added drag-and-drop stub for future enablement. Added first Playwright smoke test to establish testing pattern.

goals: Implement Cannon movement and capture logic (the 'screen' rule)
Implement Horse movement with leg-block rule and visual blocking indicators
Implement Elephant movement with eye-block and river restrictions
Write Vitest unit tests specifically for these edge-case heavy pieces
sprint_plan.deliverable: All pieces move accurately according to their individual physical constraints.

goals: Implement 'Flying General' rule (Generals cannot face each other on an open file)
Upgrade General-safety to full simulation: apply move -> validate not in check
Implement stalemate detection (treated as a loss for the side with no legal moves — add code comment noting this differs from chess draw rules)
Implement a random-legal-move bot as the simplest possible AI opponent
Add basic minimax AI (depth 2-3) with material evaluation for solo practice
Write Vitest unit tests for check validations and edge cases
sprint_plan.deliverable: A complete, rule-perfect Cờ tướng engine enforcing General-safety, playable solo against a basic AI.
sprint_plan.changes_from_original: Added random bot and simple minimax AI to enable solo play by end of week 4.

goals: Define Cờ tướng FEN string format to compress board states
Implement Undo/Redo by pushing FEN strings to a Zustand array (preventing memory bloat)
Add move history display using chosen notation convention
Document notation choice: decide between Western algebraic (Files A-I, Ranks 0-9) vs WXF standard and commit to one, with rationale documented in codebase
sprint_plan.deliverable: Robust, memory-efficient state management with undo/redo and documented notation convention.
sprint_plan.changes_from_original: Reduced scope from original Sprint 4 — moved educational UI to Sprint 5. Added notation convention decision point.

goals: Add a bottom-sheet 'Why illegal?' explanation panel with English rule snippets
Implement long-press on a piece to open a 'How this piece moves' card
Add contextual tips for common beginner mistakes (e.g., Cannon capture without screen, Horse leg block)
Polish move history panel for readability on mobile
sprint_plan.deliverable: An educational UI layer that teaches Cờ tướng rules in context as you play.
sprint_plan.changes_from_original: New sprint — extracted educational UI from the overloaded original Sprint 4.

goals: Add a curated puzzle set (JSON format): mate-in-1, mate-in-2, win-material
Create a mobile puzzle UI: goal text, reset, hint, and solution reveal
Implement shareable puzzle links by safely Base64-encoding FEN strings into the URL
Add progress tracking using localStorage (completed puzzles, attempts) with fallback for Safari private browsing
Document future migration path: URL-encoded progress string for cross-device portability without a server
sprint_plan.deliverable: A standalone, highly shareable puzzle mode that teaches Cờ tướng patterns quickly.
sprint_plan.changes_from_original: Added localStorage fallback handling and documented serverless portability path for progress data.

mobile_ui_notes.viewport_and_layout: touch-action: manipulation prevents double-tap zoom while preserving pinch-to-zoom for accessibility. Strict use of 100dvh to prevent bottom-sheet hiding on iOS Safari.
mobile_ui_notes.performance: CSS transform: translate used exclusively for piece rendering to ensure 60fps on older iPhones.
mobile_ui_notes.accessibility: ARIA labels on all interactive SVG elements. Pinch-to-zoom preserved for low-vision users. Minimum 44x44px tap targets.
mobile_ui_notes.drag_and_drop: Pointer event handlers stubbed in Sprint 2, can be enabled as a toggle in a future sprint without architectural changes.

ruleset_notes.state_history: Moves are not reverse-calculated. Entire board states are saved as lightweight FEN string snapshots.
ruleset_notes.legality: Enforced via state simulation. Stalemate equals loss (per standard Cờ tướng rules — differs from chess).
ruleset_notes.notation: Convention to be decided in Sprint 4: Western algebraic (A-I, 0-9) vs WXF standard. Decision and rationale to be documented.
ruleset_notes.ai_opponent: Random-legal-move bot available from Sprint 3B. Minimax (depth 2-3, material eval) for meaningful solo practice.
